title: carex subumbellata
intro: Carex subumbellata, also known as the cespitose sedge, is a type of perennial grass-like plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory distress, and possibly lowering blood pressure. Carex subumbellata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine, though its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
